tier_1.title: Ensure root user umask is configured
tier_1.message: No explicit umask configuration found in /root/.profile or /root/.bashrc. Default may be too permissive.
tier_1.vulnerability: A permissive root umask means newly created root files are world-readable by default.

tier_1.title: Ensure password expiration is 365 days or less
tier_1.vulnerability: Without a maximum age, a compromised credential remains valid indefinitely with no forced rotation.

tier_1.title: Ensure inactive password lock is 30 days or less
tier_1.message: INACTIVE is not configured or disabled.
tier_1.vulnerability: Dormant accounts are ideal targets because legitimate owners don't notice when they're abused.

tier_1.title: Ensure password length is 14 or more
tier_1.vulnerability: Password length is the single most impactful factor in resistance to brute-force and offline dictionary attacks.

tier_1.title: Ensure password changing requires at least 2 different characters
tier_1.vulnerability: Without difok, a user can change 'password1' to 'password2' — meaningless rotation.

tier_1.title: Ensure maximum repeated characters is 3 or less
tier_1.vulnerability: Catches trivially weak passwords like 'aaaa1234' that meet length but not quality requirements.

tier_1.title: Ensure dictionary word checks are enabled
tier_1.vulnerability: Blocks use of common English dictionary words — first target of any password spray attack.

tier_1.title: Ensure password history is 24 or greater
tier_1.message: pam_pwhistory.so not found or remember not set.
tier_1.vulnerability: Prevents reuse of the last 24 passwords — without it a user immediately re-uses their old password.

tier_1.title: Ensure failed password attempts lockout is 5 or less
tier_1.vulnerability: Core brute-force protection — without it, an attacker can attempt unlimited passwords at login.

tier_1.title: Ensure lockout time is 15 minutes or longer
tier_1.vulnerability: A short lockout window is trivially bypassed — 15 minutes defeats automated brute-force tools.

tier_1.title: Ensure a custom sudo log file exists
tier_1.message: logfile parameter is missing from Defaults.
tier_1.vulnerability: Without a dedicated sudo log file, all sudo activity is buried in syslog and easy to miss.

tier_1.title: Ensure sudo authentication timeout is configured correctly
tier_1.vulnerability: A negative timeout value disables re-prompting entirely — an unattended terminal stays permanently escalated.

tier_1.title: Ensure access to the su command is restricted
tier_1.message: pam_wheel.so is commented out or missing.
tier_1.vulnerability: Without this restriction, any local account with a known password can su to root.

tier_2.title: Ensure root account is locked for failed password attempts
tier_2.vulnerability: Without this, root is exempt from brute-force lockout — invalidating the lockout protection entirely.

tier_2.title: Ensure password quality rules apply to root
tier_2.vulnerability: Root setting a weak password while users are forced to use strong ones defeats the entire policy.
